title: Hidden Desk Apothecary Cabinet
summary: An apothecary-style cabinet of small labeled drawers that hides a fold-down desk behind its false top drawer front — real storage with a genuine hidden-desk surprise.
description:
This cabinet reads as an ordinary bank of small apothecary drawers until the top "drawer" front folds down on hinges to reveal a flat writing surface, with the actual top compartment behind it holding pens, mail, or a laptop. The false-front desk panel is captured by a piano hinge along its bottom edge and a pair of fold-out support legs (or a simple chain stop) that swing out to hold it level when open — get this hinge and support mechanism dialed in on its own before building a single drawer below it, since it is the one moving part the whole design depends on.

The small drawers below are built with simple butt-joint boxes and false fronts, sized identically for a clean grid look, and ride on basic wood runners rather than metal slides — appropriate for their light apothecary-jar-and-supplies duty. Face frame the whole cabinet in 1x2, which is what gives the small-drawer grid its crisp reveal lines.

A solid case back and back stretchers keep the whole cabinet square, which matters more here than on an open shelf unit because the fold-down desk puts a real, repeated leverage load on the top of the case every time it's opened.

tools.note: Ripping drawer box parts and the case panels.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood, sheets
materials.note: Case sides, top, bottom, and drawer fronts.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Hinged along the bottom edge with the piano hinge.

steps.title: Build the case
steps.body: Cut the case sides, top, bottom, and fixed shelf dividers. Drill pocket holes and assemble the case, checking square as you go. Attach the plywood back.

steps.title: Build the face frame
steps.body: Build and attach the 1x2 face frame to the front of the case with pocket holes and glue, leaving the top opening for the fold-down desk section.

steps.title: Fit the fold-down desk front
steps.body: Attach the piano hinge to the bottom edge of the desk front panel and to the case opening. Install the fold-out support legs so the panel is supported level when open. Confirm smooth operation before building the drawers below.

steps.title: Build the small drawers
steps.body: Build the drawer boxes with butt joints, glue, and brad nails, and attach the drawer bottoms. Install wood drawer runners in the case, then attach the drawer fronts to the boxes with pulls centered.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and drawer fronts, then prime and paint or stain and seal the whole cabinet.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Apothecary drawer cabinet with a hidden fold-down desk
